entry.title: Internal error (3998) occurred [Message 131-183] when adding document in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello,

We update a test company we had for SAP B1 v10 with a backup of the live one so we could use it as a sandbox for some things we wanted to test but when trying to create documents in this test company (sales orders, business partners, warehouses etc) we are getting the error: Internal error (3998) occurred [Message 131-183].

After restoring the database we performed the "refresh database user" in the SLD. That database user seems to have all the correct permissions the the SQL server, as compared with the users of other databases.

Any ideas as to what might be causing this?

Thank you.

Spyros

entry.title: Account determination error for pricing element in a service order
entry.content:
Hello, experts,

We are configuring the service module for S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, and running tests with service orders using advanced execution (MSO1).

Due to process requirements, we need to add some price conditions in the service order, as shown below:

However, when we try to save the order, we encounter the following error:

This error occurs for each price condition. The sales organization mentioned in the error is actually the one we created during the configuration of the service organizational unit, and in the SPRO transaction, we have already assigned this sales organization for services to the respective sales organization in sales (SD):

Additionally, in transaction VKOA, the account determination has been set up for the sales organization of SD (1002). How can we configure the system so that the account determination considers the sales organization (SD) instead of the service organization?

I would greatly appreciate any assistance with this issue.

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
David Avila.

You can find further information from the following Blog Post:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/generate-pdf-forms-in-s-4-hana-public-cloud-developer-extensibility-using/bc-p/13891203#M169636 

Unfortunately, I havent' had a chance to try it out since I'm in a hurry completing some other developments.

I hope it helps.

Regards.

entry.title: How to Configure Gmail and Office 365 for Email Notifications Sent by the Alert Service
entry.content:
In this article, we walk you through examples of the required configuration steps using Google Gmail or Microsoft Office 365 as the mail server for automated email notifications sent by the Alert Service in SAP Digital Manufacturing, but you can use other products.

Introduction

The Alert Service in SAP Digital Manufacturing aims to notify production operators or supervisors about events or exceptions happening on the shop floor and intentionally introduce human decision making when responding to them. Alerts can either be created manually within Production Operator Dashboards (PODs) or automatically by production processes. Alerts contain configurable data fields (also known as alert properties) which include the context information that led to the creation of the alert. For example, they can contain information such as plant, work center, resource, operation, material, SFC, order, or readings from sensors connected via shopfloor systems or MQTT brokers. The alert service can be configured to send out in-app and/or email notifications to a list of recipients upon alert generation, status or processor change. For more information about the alert service and some example end-to-end scenarios, please refer to my earlier blogs Create an Issue in SAP Quality Issue Resolution from an Alert in SAP Digital Manufacturing, Enriching Readings from MQTT Sensors with the Applicable Manufacturing Context, the Alerts section of the Application Help for Execution and the Enabling Alert Services section of the Operations Guide for SAP Digital Manufacturing in the SAP Help Portal.

Before the alert service can send out email notifications, some configuration is required as outlined in the Configure Email Notifications section of the Operations Guide for SAP Digital Manufacturing. In this context, however, it is important to know that service providers often have specific requirements for port settings and two-factor authentication to access the SMTP server. Therefore, I’ll explain the configuration in more detail along the two examples of Gmail and Office 365 so that you can avoid some of the pitfalls and have the relevant links to the documentation of the two service providers at hand. Hopefully, these examples will help you while setting up the alert service for email notifications.

 

Using Gmail for Email Notifications

Start with setting up a destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit with the following values:

Property Name

Property Value

Notes

Name

SAP_DM_NOTIFICATIONS_MAIL

 

Type

Mail

 

Proxy Type

Internet

 

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

 

User

 

Gmail email address

Password

 

"App password" created in Gmail
as explained below

mail.smtp.host

smtp.gmail.com

 

mail.smtp.port

587

 

mail.transport.protocol

smtp

 

mail.smtp.from

 

Gmail email address

mail.smtp.starttls.enable

true

 

In Gmail, execute the following two steps as explained in the respective links:

  1. Turn on 2-Step Verification on the Gmail account.

  2. Create an app password from your Gmail account. This password is required in the destination configuration in SAP BTP Cockpit.

 

Using Office 365 for Email Notifications

Start with setting up a destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit with the following values:

 

Property Name

Property Value

Notes

Name

SAP_DM_NOTIFICATIONS_MAIL

 

Type

Mail

 

Proxy Type

Internet

 

Authentication

OAuth2JWTBearer

 

Client ID

 

Set up an app registration in Azure. The client id is provided by this app registration as explained below.

Client Secret

 

Create a secret in the app registration and use the secret value here as explained below.

Token Service URL

https://login.microsoftonline.com/<O365-tenant-id>/oauth2/v2.0/token

This is the URL to fetch OAuth2 tokens for Office 365. You need your Office 365 tenant ID.

mail.smtp.host

smtp.office365.com

 

mail.smtp.port

587

 

mail.transport.protocol

smtp

 

mail.smtp.from

 

This is the email address of the user in Office 365 that is used for sending.
This value, along with the token, is used to authenticate to the Office 365 mail server.

mail.smtp.auth.mechanisms

XOAUTH2

This property forces the authentication to the mail server to use
XOauth2 protocol as documented in this article section of Microsoft Learn.

mail.smtp.oauth.scope

https://outlook.office365.com/.default

This property is needed when the token provider requires "scope" as a parameter to
be included in the request to create a token. For Office 365, the value should be set to https://outlook.office365.com/.default
For more details, see this article section in Microsoft Learn.

The screenshot below displays a destination configured for Office 365. You need to provide proper values from "Client ID", "Client Secret", "Token Service URL", and "mail.smtp.from".

The remaining steps of the configuration are all done in Office 365:

  1. Create an App Registration in Azure or Microsoft Entra: The app registration is used to provide the client ID and client secret for OAuth2 authentication. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn. 

  2. Create a Client Secret for App Registration: Create a secret for the App Registration from the previous section. Note that only client secrets are currently supported and mTLS isn't supported. The client ID and secret are required in the destination configuration in SAP BTP Cockpit. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn. 

  3. Add Required Permission to App Registration: Add the following permission to the app registration: Office 365 Exchange Online → SMTP.SendAsApp
    This permission configuration is completed in the Applications → Enterprise Applications section of Microsoft Entra.

    The screenshot below shows the app registration "SMTP-send" granted the required permission. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn. 
  4. Grant Tenant Admin Consent in Azure: The tenant admin has to provide consent to the permission change done in the previous step. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn. When you have a single tenant in Office 365, you can accomplish this by choosing the "Grant admin consent" button above the permission list. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn.

  5. Add the User used to Send Mail to the App Registration: Identify a technical user to be used as the mail account to send email notifications. This user needs to be added to the app registration. The below screenshot shows a technical user called "Technical user" added to the app registration "SMTP-send" with the user role.
  6. Add the App Registration to Exchange Online: This step requires the use of Exchange online PowerShell. For more information, see this article section in Microsoft Learn.

  7. Provide the Mailbox Permission to App Registration in the User Account for Sending Emails: For more information, again, see this article section in Microsoft Learn. 

  8. Ensure that "Authenticated SMTP" is Enabled for the User Account: In the Microsoft 365 Online Admin center, open the active users and select the user account used to send emails. On the "Mail" tab, ensure that "All apps allowed" is under the Email Apps section. By default, "Authenticated SMTP" isn't selected, but it is required.

This completes the required configurations steps for Office 365.

entry.title: Sequencing/Scheduling with user defined fields in S4 PPDS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Need help to understand which is the feasible options to consider below mentioned scheduling/sequencing scenario

We have a scenario where we do sequencing of order based on user defined fields that do not appear on the sort list.

 

1. Work with routing classification - 018 Class type (Without Block planning)

For this we wanted to use Routing classification at operation level by defining the user defined attributes as characteristics in this routing class. But we do not want to use this routing classification for Block planning. We want to use these characteristics for a time continuous resource sequencing. Please let us know if this is a feasible approach.

 

2. Consider Routing operation User field Key:

Also apart from the above option we are also considering usage of routing operation user field keys to define these attributes as user fields. And use the CIF BAPIs to send these user defined fields to PPDS and make them populate them at order level and add them in the sort list of sequencing heuristics.

 

3. Use Material Mater Characteristics - Material Class:

Use Material classification by defining the user defined attributes as characteristics in material class. CIF these relevant characteristics to PPDS and make them populate at Order level and add them in the sort list of sequencing heuristics

__PRESENT

entry.title: Text association creating new column
entry.content:
I create a new CDS view to field as semantic text:

define view entity zt001 as select from t001
{
  key bukrs,
      @Semantics.text: true
      butxt
}

 

After that, i created a CDS view to expose data with text association:

define view entity ztest as select from t001
  association [0..1] to zt001 as _zt001 on $projection.bukrs = _zt001.bukrs
{
    @ui.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
    @ObjectModel.text.association: '_zt001'
    key bukrs,
    _zt001
}

 

When i execute it, the extra column with description of company code is generated:

 

I try to remove association at end of CDS ztest, but the text association doesn't work in this case.

How can i remove this column "Company name"?

entry.title: Re: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
Hi @NareshDasika18,

this naming convention is meant for multi tier environment, where you have separated tenant for dev/test and prod.

For customers, which do have only one tenant (used both as dev/test and prod) we add letter T or P after SCI. So my example 

SCI00200150_IN_VeloCorp_HTTP_SFTP_OrderRequest

will become

SCIT00200150_IN_VeloCorp_HTTP_SFTP_OrderRequest

or 

SCIP00200150_IN_VeloCorp_HTTP_SFTP_OrderRequest

Mind the 4th character T or P. In that case we also maintain two packages for each project one for test and one for prod. Same with endpoints on Sender adapters (like http).

entry.title: Re: Scan a directory on application server with LONG file names
entry.content:
I had problems with both RZL_READ_DIR_LOCAL and EPS2_GET_DIRECTORY_LISTING.

RZL_READ_DIR_LOCAL returns only 32 characters, and EPS2_GET_DIRECTORY_LISTING has authorization issues.

I was able to solve this by using CL_FILE_UTIL=>readdir, as per Note 3273137

entry.title: Re: Batch number not getting copied in Return order
entry.content:
So you are creating a Return Order with reference to Billing Document and Batch number is not copied. The reason is in the Invoice you only have main Item. 

This mean the Batch Items are not available in the Invoice and when Return Order is created, main item from Invoice is referred. obviously the Batch number will not be available in Returns Order, unless you have Batch Items in the Invoice and they are referred while creating Returns Order. 

If you do not want to change the process, only option would be to have a Custom Copy Control routine which will fetch the Batch number from Delivery Document (via Table VBFA). However what would happen incase there are multiple Batch items for one Main Item?

Dear Community,

Save the Date!  Tuesday, October 22, 2024.  We are happy to announce our upcoming 2411 release webcast for SAP Sales and Service Cloud (Cloud for Customer).  We will be adding the release webcast-related information and assets here in our community. This will allow you to access the on-demand recording after the live event, short demos if available, slide deck, and also keep the conversation alive.

SAP Sales and Service Cloud / Cloud for Customer – 2411 Release Briefing Webcast

  • When: Tuesday, October 22, 2024 @ 4:00 – 5:00 pm CEST/ 10:00am -11:00 am EDT
  • Who: Presented by Emmanuel Desert and Sales & Service Cloud Product Managers
  • What: Join us for an insightful preview of the planned innovations for the upcoming November 2024 (2411) Release of SAP Sales & Service Cloud. We will talk about highlights in Platform & Integration for SAP Sales and Service Cloud.

Visit the documentation page to check out the current updates.
Please bookmark our 2024 release schedule located here.

entry.title: Re: Create CDS Entity in system 2 based on table in system 1
entry.content:
Hi @nagaraosunkara 

You can try to solve your problem by creating a custom table, where the data will be populated by a job scheduling (how to create you can find here) that will perform the following logic:

1 Read data from system 1;
2 Populate the custom table in system 2;

Set the job to trigger at an interval, for example, every 1 minute.

After that, create an CDS based on the custom table.

I hope this helps.

BR,
Oleg

entry.title: Re: Different server groups for different queues in SMQR
entry.content:
Hello,

According to SAP Note 2255373 it is possible to achieve this using the "Destination with LOGON Data" column in SMQR transaction.

Refer to SAP Note 2255373 - XI runtime:  XI message is not processed on logon server/host.

With best regards.

entry.title: Re: From ECC to S/4HANA Public Cloud Refactor (“Re-architect”)
entry.content:
In your situation I would do the following:

  1. Analyze usage of custom code.
  2. Replace custom code with standard (Fit-to-standard).
  3. Investigate if and which Low-Code/No-Code tools can be used:
    1. Key user extensibility
    2. SAP Build Apps
    3. SAP Build Process Automation
  4. For the remaining gaps, investigate which option for developer extensibility is the right one:
    1. On-stack
    2. Side-by-side

Some important advantage of using RAP / ABAP Cloud:
Can be used for on-stack and side-by-side extensions. CAP is only available for side-by-side extensions.

Some further reading:

  • ABAP Call Monitor (SCMON) – Analyze usage of your ... - SAP Community
  • SAP S/4HANA Extensibility – Simplified Guide for B... - SAP Community
  • Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions
  • RAP vs CAP - Key Differences between the Two Progr... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @Ashok_R, I got IT to deploy the add-in for us today and had a look. Essentially the feature can be described as follows: The add-in accesses a SAC dashboard, renders a widget and copies a screenshot of the rendered widget onto a PowerPoint slide. It's a neat feature that could work well in hybrid scenarios where a client has a comprehensive management deck in PowerPoint that you haven't fully automated yet.

First, I did notice today that I'm unable to refresh any widget though. When I look into the add-in, all buttons - Sign Out, Add Widget, Refresh Data - are greyed out. Sign In is active and opens the add-in. Help and About are also active. Is this a bug or am I missing a configuration somewhere? entry.title: Localization in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
SAP is well-known for its best practices, and for S/4HANA Public Cloud customers, this comes with the benefit of pre-configured settings that allow for running business transactions across various lines of business. As businesses expand globally, it becomes increasingly important for Cloud ERP solutions to meet the specific business and legal requirements of different countries and regions.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition offers a comprehensive list of countries/regions with specific functions in addition to general lines of business functionalities. Last time I checked, there are 59 out of box localized country/region versions. The latest updates to the country/region list can be found on the SAP Help Portal.

For customers participating in early adopter programs, the Configuration Localization Tool (CLT) from SAP Central Business Configuration allows for copying Germany as a template to about 160 countries that are not part of SAP delivered local versions. You can find the latest information in SAP note. 

Moreover, the Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/⁠4HANA Cloud provides tools and frameworks that enable the extension of country/region-specific functions for SAP-delivered local versions and customer own local versions.  Customers can extend their SAP S/⁠4HANA Cloud system using key user extensibility, side-by-side extensibility, and developer extensibility, including the multi-off-delivery option.

The Activate roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) includes a dedicated Localization tag to provide an end-to-end view of the topic. This encompasses selecting countries/regions scope during Digital Discovery Assessment in Discover phase, self-enabling of SAP delivered local versions, configuration tool and modern extension capability during the Prepare phase, and capturing countries/regions specific requirements for configuration setting adjustments and extension developments during the Explore phase. And, in the Realize phase of the project, use SAP Central Business Configuration to set up new countries/regions, and leverage Localization as Self Service, including both CLT and extensibility capabilities, to further extend and adapt. 

In conclusion, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offers a comprehensive and flexible solution for businesses operating globally, ensuring compliance with specific country and region requirements while also providing the tools for further adaptation and extension.

Stay tuned for more updates and blogs.

Follow the SAP Activate page on the SAP Community. Remember to turn on your notification to receive updates or information about items requiring your attention. To enable notifications, follow the steps on the SAP Community Resources page.

SAP Activate Minute

Tune into the SAP Activate Minute videos in our Playlist here. Subscribe to get notified when we drop new videos. Here is the one for Localization.

entry.title: Re: No Where-Used List for the Authorization Object in SE...
entry.content:
Due to certain restriction with the Where-Used List, as it requires a number of special search areas that are not available in the standard where-used list, this function was moved to the tool bar of transaction SE80/SU21.

In earlier version of the system, the Where-used List can be found under Additional information.

In the newer releases, you can use the new buttons 'Customizing Objects' and 'Development Objects', thy provide the same functions as the older version with new functionalities as well.

'Customizing Objects' can perform the same functions as the previous Where-used List while 'Development Objects' will ran a background job with the determined filters for the authorization object selected.

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of Career Development Planning
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 2H 2024 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 2H 2024 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- AI-Assisted Career Insights:

Leveraging generative AI capabilities, employees can gain insights into development actions they can take towards their target role.

In the latest Career Worksheet, employees can access the feature by choosing the Generate button in the new Boost your career with AI-powered insights! a section on a target role's details page. Generative AI produces insights by considering several factors, including employees' current role information, their skill and attribute ratings, and the description, required skills, and expected ratings of the target role.

Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest Career Worksheet.
  • You've purchased the AI units license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • You've enabled the AI Services Administration Assisted Career Insights option.
  • You've consented to the AI Usage Acknowledgment Statement.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrators have the Administrator Manage AI Capabilities AI Services Administration permission.
  • Employees have the User AI Access Assisted Career Insights permission.

2- Allow More Roles to Launch Role Readiness Forms in Career Worksheet:

You can now configure the Career Worksheet template to allow more roles such as manager and HR to launch role readiness forms for an employee’s target role. Previously, only the employees themselves could launch such forms.

With the new <permission for="launch-assessment-form"> setting configured in the Career Worksheet template, roles who are granted this permission can see the Evaluate Readiness link in Career Worksheet. By choosing the link, they launch a role readiness form for a specific job role, and the form will be routed according to its route map until completion. Here's a screenshot of the feature.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • The feature is currently only supported in the legacy version of Career Worksheet.
  • You've enabled Performance Management and configured a form template for role readiness assessment. In this form template, you've configured settings to allow other roles to launch forms for the employee role.
  • You've linked the form template with Career Worksheet in the Career Worksheet template.
  • You've configured the <permission for="launch-assessment-form"> setting in the Career Worksheet template.

3- Change Participants in In-Progress Mentoring Programs:

Mentoring administrators and program owners can now remove mentors and mentees from in-progress open enrollment and unsupervised programs in Admin Center Manage Mentoring Programs. They're also allowed to add mentors and mentees to in-progress unsupervised programs. Previously, they were only allowed to add or remove participants for supervised programs.

Enablement: Automatically on

When added or removed, mentors and mentees receive corresponding email notifications, including the following:

  • Mentoring Invitation to Additional Mentors
  • Mentoring Invitation to Additional Mentees
  • Mentor/Mentee Removal Notification

Administrators can customize the email notifications in Admin Center Email Notification Templates Settings.

4- Enhancements to Latest Development Goals:

In the latest version of Development Goals, users can now view audit history for custom learning activities and upload certificates to custom learning activities.

Every time a learning activity is created or modified, an audit history record is generated. With proper permissions, users can see a list of such records showing what changes were made, who made the changes, and when. If a learning activity has been modified, users can see a comparison between the previous values and the new values of the changed fields.

Note that linking a custom learning activity to a development goal is not logged in the learning activity's audit history.

Enablement: Customer Configured

With the certifications field configured in the learning activity template, users can upload one or two certificates to a completed custom learning activity. Here's a screenshot of the feature.

Configuration Requirements:

  • The latest Goal Management is enabled.
  • Transcript learning is enabled and users can create custom learning activities.
  • (For learning audit history) Users have read permission to the learning activity fields in the learning activity template.
  • (For learning certificates) The certifications field is configured in the learning activity template and users have the read and write permissions to this field.

5- Manage Administrator Permissions for Mentoring Programs:

By default, mentoring administrators have permission to manage all mentoring programs in your organization. You can set up permission to control which administrators are authorized to manage specific programs.

To do this, you can create administrator permission in Manage Mentoring Programs>Settings >Administrator Permission Configuration, selecting a mentoring program and the target administrators who can manage it.

Configuration Requirements:

  • You need to configure a new permission to use the feature. See Role-Based Permission Prerequisites.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • You have the Administrator>Manage Career Development>Manage Mentoring Programs permission.
  • You have the Administrator>Manage Career Development>Manage Administrator Permissions for Programs permission.

6- Mentoring Matched Date Available in Reporting:

You can now report on the matched date of mentors and mentees in mentoring programs in Table and Story reports.

The Matched Date column in the reports shows the date when mentors and mentees were first matched. The date won't be changed in the following cases:
  • Mentors or mentees restart the mentoring relationship after they've ended it
  • Mentoring administrators remove matching between mentors and mentees

Enablement: Automatically on

7- Prevent Removal of Learning Activities from Completed Development Goals:

In the latest version of Development Goals, you can now prevent users from removing learning activities after their development goals are completed. Previously, there was no such restriction.

If you enable the Prevent removal of learning activities from completed development goals setting in a development goal plan template, users are not allowed to remove learning added from SAP SuccessFactors Learning or custom learning activities from completed development goals.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • The latest Goal Management is enabled.
  • You've enabled the Prevent removal of learning activities from completed development goals setting in Manage Templates  Development  <development goal plan template>  General Settings.

8- View More Information About Roles in Latest Career Worksheet:

In the latest version of Career Worksheet, users can now view the job family, incumbents, and organizational career paths of their current role, target roles, and recommended roles.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest Career Worksheet.
  • For viewing the current employees of a role, make sure that the Employee Directory is selected in Provisioning and the cws-people-role attribute in the Career Worksheet template is true, which is the default value.
  • If Employee Directory isn't selected in Provisioning, users can only view the number of current employees, but can't choose [Number] Employees in This Role to view the detailed information.
    For viewing the career paths of a role, make sure that Enable Career Path V2 is selected in Provisioning.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • To view the current employees of a role, users must have the User>General User Permission>Company Info Access>User Search permission, and a defined target population.
  • For viewing the career paths of a role, users must have the View permission to any of the following permissions depending on your system:
  • If the system was migrated from Job Profile Builder to Talent Intelligence Hub, the permission is User>Miscellaneous Permissions>Career Path.
  • If the system was migrated from Job Description Manager to Talent Intelligence Hub, the permission is User>Miscellaneous Permissions>Legacy Career Path.

Here are just a few of the changes included in the latest release. For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Re: In the "Purge Master Data" job, is there any option to get an attachment file with &qu
entry.content:
Hi Sonia,

Thanks for the question.

There is a preview mode for the Purge Master Data job.
In that case a file will be attached to the job log informing you about the master data that would get deleted.

Please see here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/feae3cea3cc549aaa9d9de7d363a83e6/ebbf495346e14c39b965742d713c0238.html?q=purge+master+data

Please keep in mind that the list is not comprehensive. The preview will not contain related master data, like compound master data that would get deleted as well. 
For example, if you are running the job in preview mode for purging locations, the attachment will contain only locations that would be deleted in a normal run. The attachment will not contain however the location-product master data. When running the purge master data job in a normal run, purging locations would result also in removing some dependent location-products due to the deleted locations.

Kind regards,
Zoltan Biro.

entry.title: Data Replication straight to SAP PAPM Cloud UM's Model Entity via Hana Cloud FlowGraph
entry.content:
Friendly Reminder:

This is a technical blogpost and you should work with your Business Application Studio experts in performing the steps provided below to successfully do Source to Model Entity replication via Flowgraph

 

In SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud’s Universal Model (SAP PAPM Cloud UM) release in 2023 with a purpose of supporting transactional processing, we cannot avoid discussing if there are possibilities to do Data Replication.

 

What is Data Replication?

To those that are new to this terminology, let me explain in the easiest form --- Data Replication is the cloning or copying of data from an external source table to a replica table to ensure that you always have the most accurate and latest data in your system locally for transactional processing without being too dependent to the external source’s availability. This means that even if the source becomes unavailable or the connection got intermittent, business’ transactional processing is not to suffer or get affected by this unavailability as the same data needed from the external source is already accessible via the replica tables.


Still vague? Let me show you via visual representation. Imagine Live Connection…. 

In live connection, the system relies heavily on Source and Connection availability

This means if the source or connection suddenly becomes unavailable, processing will also get interrupted due to missing information needed.

This type of issue is usually being realized in transactional processing since data is heavily accessed more often due to more frequent process execution. As a solution to this dilemma, Data Replication has always been a disaster recovery plan for such situations and challenges.


In data replication mode, while the source is up, replication or fetching of data happens Realtime or Batch (scheduled) from the external source to safeguard information all the time in the Replica Table (which is locally available).

This helps in case the source or connection is down, transactional processing can still happen via the locally available replica table.

I hope my explanation above helped you visualize Data Replication. But to know more about Data Replication in HANA Cloud, see SAP HANA Cloud’s Data Replication Guide. You may also follow this useful developer’s guide from SAP concerning Remote Table Replication.

Fast forward to now that you already know how to do SAP HANA Cloud Data Replication, let us proceed with doing a more sophisticated Data Replication process. Our mission together in this blogpost is to consume data from an external HANA 2.0 Database (via Smart Data Integration) or HANA Cloud Database (Smart Data Access), then create a replication process. However, this time we use SAP PAPM UM’s Model Entity as a Replica Table. Sounds great and useful right so you can use directly the data from your source to SAP PAPM Cloud UM without the need for connection?

Since this mission is to replicate information in an existing table (SAP PAPM UM’s Model Entity), normal replication process via Remote Table Replication is not to work as we need to respect the fields available in the Model Entity including its technical fields so it will be read by SAP PAPM Universal Model solution as an artifact. Let me show you what I meant.

If we are to use the normal replication option, you will see that our Model Entity Table looks something like this upon SAP PAPM Cloud UM activation

As a normal replication process you will need to then perform below SQL commands in your SAP PAPM Cloud UM database in order for the replication to kick in

 

 

 

 

 

 

-- TO CREATE A REMOTE SUBSCRIPTION
CREATE REMOTE SUBSCRIPTION <REMOTESUBSCRIPTIONNAME> ON <VIRTUALTABLENAME> TARGET TABLE <TARGETTABLENAME>;

-- TO START THE REPLICATION PROCESS
ALTER REMOTE SUBSCRIPTION <REMOTESUBSCRIPTIONNAME> DISTRIBUTE;

 

 

 

 

You can see that the original table got updated and the _FIELDS were all removed by this replication process. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Since this step will cause a structure change to the generated Model Entity Table, it is unfortunately will lead to possible corruption of the table with respect to using it in SAP PAPM Cloud UM's show screen for example

Due to that we cannot make use of direct replication approach if we want to write to a Model Entity specially if the fields of the source and the target Model Entity are not  having the same structure or fields. 

So what if I want to ensure that the fields of the Model Entity stays as is, and still be able to do replication even if my source fields are not 1:1 with my Model Entity? How can we handle that situation? We will use another approach of replication with the help of Flowgraph. 

I am introducing lots of things in this blogpost, am I not? (^^,) . Let me explain to you what is Flowgraph


What is Flowgraph?

In the most simplest form, FlowGraph is a HANA development functionality that be accessed via Business Application Studio which then helps HANA developers with data transformation and replication with visual representation.

Here is an example on how to create a flowgraph via Business Application Studio

 

 

Here is an example of a very simple Data Source > Data Target Flowgraph creation

 

I hope by now you are convinced that it is pretty easy to use. And since flowgraph only performs pushing wihtout adjusting your target table, this is the best replication method we can use to populate directly the datarecords in our Model Entity. 

Just to straighten expectations, definitely there is more to Flowgraph that I will not be covering in this blogpost, but for the meantime let us focus on using this to replicate source information directly to Model Entity of Universal Model in a form of upsert and in a form of scheduled replication. To avoid confusion, let us divide our topics into 3 chapters:

  1. Installation and Subscription
  2. Authorization Grants
  3. Replication via Flowgraph using simple data

A)  Installation and Subscription

Just to help, I placed here all the important prerequisites to be able to use Flowgraph successfully. In case you already have an established systems and BAS setup, feel free to skip this chapter and proceed with B) Authorization Grants

A1)  Create Subaccount

Ensure to have a commercial (non-trial) global account where you can create a subaccount where the cloud applications can be subscribed on.

Once the subaccount subscription has been completed, do not forget to assign the BTP roles related to subaccount administration so you can perform the next subscription processes


A2)  Subscribe to SAP HANA Cloud (Plan: Tool)

Subscribe to SAP HANA Cloud: Tool to be able to subscribe completely to a standalone SAP HANA Cloud.

Upon subscribing to SAP HANA Cloud Tool, below roles will be introduced. Do not forget to assign these BTP roles related to SAP HANA Cloud to perform the HANA Cloud Database subscription and database administration


A3)  Subscribe to SAP HANA Cloud (Plan: hana)

Since we need to use BAS, as a prerequisite, a standalone or subscribed SAP HANA Cloud is important to be sitting in the same Subaccount. It is important to understand that this is NOT the same as the runtime database of SAP PAPM Cloud.


A4)  Subscribe to SAP PAPM Cloud (Plan: Default or BYOD)

Since our goal is to write or clone data directly to SAP PAPM UM’s Model Entity, a SAP PAPM Cloud subscription with activated SAP PAPM Cloud UM obviously is needed.

After subscription, and in case you want to follow through the simple example below.
In SAP PAPM UM, create an environment with at least one Model entity

For example

ENVIRONMENT ID

FLOWGRAPH

FIELD1

CUSTOMER, Characteristic - String

FIELD2

PRODUCT, Characteristic - String

FIELD3

QUANTITY, Keyfigure - Integer

FIELD4

Amount, Keyfigure - Integer

FUNCTION ID

FG_ME

FUNCTION FIELDS

CUSTOMER (KEY), PRODUCT, QUANTITY, AMOUNT

Disclaimer: The Model Entity can have more fields, for the meantime I use a simple one with a sole purpose of showing you how to replicate data from external source to the model entity directly

A5)  Subscribe to Business Application Studio (Standard Edition)

BAS will serve as the application to create flowgraph in a specific HDI Container. Do not forget to assign the BTP roles related to BAS to perform HANA Development via BAS


A6)  Establish Connection between BAS and SAP HANA Cloud

As this is a full course, I will skip showing the step-by-step procedure for this. But to summarize you need to make sure that you perform below steps while watching the SAP Academy Video.                

  • Dev Space Creation, SAP HANA Native Application
  • HANA DB Project Creation
  • Establish HANA DB Connection + Create HANA shared-HDI container
  • Create HANA Service Instance
  • Hdbgrant and yaml file creation

    VERY IMPORTANT NOTE:

    It is mandatory that you are comfortable in working at Business Application Studio (BAS) as almost all steps that you are to encounter in C) Create Flowgraph using Simple Data are to be done in BAS.


A7)  Create Remote Source in SAP HANA Cloud

In the BAS linked SAP HANA Cloud, create multiple remote sources to connect to your On-Premise or Cloud databases. Imagine that these sources are the connections that would be used by flowgraph to pull data from Source tables via Smart Data Access or Smart Data Integration.

B)  Authorization Grants

Now that we are done with A) Installation and Subscription, it is now time to update some database authorization for smooth connectivity and data consumption. In this chapter we are to give proper authorization to the HDI Container users to read external artifacts, as well as giving HDI Container administrative rights to the Service Instance user.

Note: In case you are not familiar of these users, I really suggest that you revisit the BAS training in Chapter A6) Establish Connection between BAS and SAP HANA Cloud to understand who is the HDI Container user as well as the ServiceInstance user. These users play a very important role in creating artifacts in the database including the flowgraph.

 

B1)  ServiceInstance user to have Admin rights to the BAS HDI Container

In the BAS connected database, execute the following SQL Commands to give the Service Instance user access to the HDI container to create artifacts if need be.

In the HANA SQL script below:

  • DBADMIN is the BAS related Instance User
  • FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1 is the BAS related HDI Container

 

 

 

 

 

 

--Login as any user such as DBADMIN or HDI Container Owner
--option 1: choose which privilege to give
CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY COLUMN TABLE #PRIVILEGES LIKE _SYS_DI.TT_SCHEMA_PRIVILEGES;
INSERT INTO #PRIVILEGES ( PRIVILEGE_NAME, PRINCIPAL_SCHEMA_NAME, PRINCIPAL_NAME ) VALUES ( 'CREATE ANY', '', 'DBADMIN' );
INSERT INTO #PRIVILEGES ( PRIVILEGE_NAME, PRINCIPAL_SCHEMA_NAME, PRINCIPAL_NAME ) VALUES ( 'SELECT', '', 'DBADMIN' );
INSERT INTO #PRIVILEGES ( PRIVILEGE_NAME, PRINCIPAL_SCHEMA_NAME, PRINCIPAL_NAME ) VALUES ( 'UPDATE', '', 'DBADMIN' );
CALL FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#DI.GRANT_CONTAINER_SCHEMA_PRIVILEGES( #PRIVILEGES, _SYS_DI.T_NO_PARAMETERS, ?, ?, ?);
DROP TABLE #PRIVILEGES;

--option 2: this is full administrator rights in the specific HDI Container
CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY TABLE #PRIVILEGES LIKE _SYS_DI.TT_API_PRIVILEGES;
INSERT INTO #PRIVILEGES (PRINCIPAL_NAME, PRIVILEGE_NAME, OBJECT_NAME) SELECT 'DBADMIN', PRIVILEGE_NAME, OBJECT_NAME FROM _SYS_DI.T_DEFAULT_DI_ADMIN_PRIVILEGES;
CALL FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#_DI.GRANT_CONTAINER_SCHEMA_PRIVILEGES( #PRIVILEGES, _SYS_DI.T_NO_PARAMETERS, ?, ?, ?);
CALL _SYS_DI.GRANT_CONTAINER_GROUP_API_PRIVILEGES('SYS_DI', #PRIVILEGES, _SYS_DI.T_NO_PARAMETERS, ?, ?, ?);
DROP TABLE #PRIVILEGES;

 

 

 

 

 

 


B2)  ServiceInstance user to have rights to adjust SAP_PAPM_UM.Z table

This granting is only needed if your SAP PAPM Cloud is on BYOD mode. This means that the database of your SAP PAPM Cloud UM and the BAS connected database are the same and you want to give your serviceinstance user an access to write to SAP PAPM UM table.

If you are using a SAP PAPM Cloud on Runtime mode, you must skip this step.

 

 

 

 

 

 

--login as SAP_PAPM_ADMIN like user and perform below step
GRANT "<UM SCHEMA>"."external_access_role#" TO DBADMIN;

 

 

 

 

 

 


B3)  HDI Container user to have access to other schemas’ artifacts

Below is just an example where HDI Container gets access to information coming from other schema, in this case DBADMIN.

In the HANA SQL script below:

  • DBADMIN schema is the artifact owner
  • FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1 users are all related BAS’ HDI container

 

 

 

 

 

 

--execute commands as schema owner, in this case DBADMIN
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON SCHEMA DBADMIN TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON SCHEMA DBADMIN TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#DI;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON SCHEMA DBADMIN TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#OO;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON SCHEMA DBADMIN TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1_xxxxxxxx_DT;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON SCHEMA DBADMIN TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1_xxxxxxxx_RT;

 

 

 

 

 

 


B4)  HDI Container user to have access to SAP PAPM UM Model Entity

This granting is only needed if your SAP PAPM Cloud is on BYOD mode. This means that the database of your SAP PAPM Cloud UM and the BAS connected database are the same and you want to give your HDI Container access to SAP PAPM UM table.

This is basically important so the HDI Container user can create synonym linked to SAP PAPM UM artifacts

In the HANA SQL script below:

  • FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1 users are all related BAS’ HDI container

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

--execute commands as DBADMIN or the ServiceInstance user that has access to SAP_PAPM_UM schema

GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#DI;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1#OO;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1_xxxxxxxx_DT;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" TO FGRAPH_BYOD_HDI_DB_1_xxxxxxxx_RT;

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Alright now we are fully set!!!

Let us proceed with the final Chapter and the main point of this blogpost, FLOWGRAPH…

C)  Create Flowgraph using simple Data

In the near future (latest Q1 or Q2 2025) there will be two productive plans available for SAP PAPM Cloud such as Default (Runtime Database) and BYOD (Bring your own database). Since these two databases have different administration rights, the steps to perform the replication would also be different.

 

I will share with you both, so you have options in the future.

C1)  OP Replicating to Runtime UM

Below steps are just example of how you can setup to push information from your On-premise database directly to your Model Entity. In here we will be using a flowgraph execution on batch mode, which we will eventually schedule to run “almost” real-time via Cronjob to fetch and store data to the model entity. Let us proceed!

 

C1.1)  In the target (runtime db) perform below statements

The goal of this script is to prepare and know if SAP_PAPM_ADMIN user actually has enough privilege to write to the Model Entity. If this fails, ensure to provide proper authorization prior performing the succeeding steps, else your flowgraph procedure will fail.

 

 

 

 

 

 

CONNECT SAP_PAPM_ADMIN PASSWORD <password>;

-- ensure that SAP_PAPM_ADMIN has enough rights to manage the UM model entity

--insert records to the model entity table
INSERT INTO "SAP_PAPM_UM"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" (CUSTOMER, PRODUCT, QUANTITY, AMOUNT) VALUES ('TEST', 'TEST', 0, 0);

--check result of the model entity table
SELECT * FROM "SAP_PAPM_UM"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME";

--delete record inserted to clear up the model entity table
DELETE FROM "SAP_PAPM_UM"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'TEST'; 

--check result of the model entity table
SELECT * FROM "SAP_PAPM_UM"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME";

 

 

 

 

 

 

Note: By default, all the steps should be ok as SAP_PAPM_ADMIN has proper rights to manage SAP PAPM UM Z tables.

 

C1.2)  From the BAS linked Database, create a remote source

The BAS linked database will act as a middleman between source and target tables. In Chapter A.7. Create Remote Source in SAP HANA Cloud we created multiple Remote sources to establish connection on the source tables; this time we are to create a remote source to establish connection to the target table (Model Entity of Universal Model).

This remote source example below should point to the PAPM Runtime Database to be able to replicate data directly to the Model Entity. It is also important or advisable that you use SAP_PAPM_ADMIN as Remote Source Technical User to avoid extra authorization issue.

  

C1.3)  From BAS, update the hdbgrant file to include the created remote source

Add the remote source (in this case PAPMRUNTIME) in both Object Owner and Application User Tabs of the hdbgrant file with atleast CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE privilege.

 

This will give your BAS application enough authorization to create virtual table from the said remote source.

C1.4)  From BAS, create a VT pointing to SAP_PAPM_UM > Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME

It is possible to create the VT (Virtual Table) outside the HDI container, but I suggest creating the VT directly in the HDI container via BAS to avoid extra steps such as synonyms creation and additional unnecessary authorization grants.

C1.5)  From BAS, create Flowgraph FG_RT01

As you can see in the image, the Flowgraph created was very simple.

It only has Data Source pointing to the Virtual Table linked to the source database, in this case it is On-Premise database. And a  Data Target pointing to the Virtual table linked to the Model Entity of SAP PAPM UM (writing option used is UPSERT). To be guided how to create flowgraph, revisit What is Flowgraph? section

C1.6)  From BAS, execute and check Result

Perform a test run to ensure that the Flowgraph configuration is as you intended. You will know if it is successful if you see the result or information from your On-Premise database to your Model Entity. The model entity result can be checked via HANA Cloud database of your SAP PAPM UM, or via Show Screen Application. 

C1.7) From BAS, Build a HANA JobScheduler

As the setup we performed uses manual execution, a cronjob or scheduled job is necessary to execute C1.6 automatically in a periodic manner which can also be called as BATCH processing. 

Before you can create a JobScheduler, you will need to know which procedure you are to call in periodically. Since a flowgraph is creating a procedure, we need to find first the procedure link to a flowgraph. Below is an example on how you can find the procedure

Once you know the procedure, you can proceed with creating the JobScheduler

Below is an example of a Cronjob scheduled to run every 30 seconds.

 

Note: The scheduling of cron should be evaluated by your database administrator. As this is just a demo, I used 30seconds. In reality, you would want to schedule this every 15minutes or every hour depending on how frequent your source table is being updated, and how much datarecords the table has.

 

C1.8) Perform some live checks

Now that you are all set, you might want to do some live check to see if the full flow works from pulling of information to writing it to your model Entity.

For example, I used simple statements like below to see if the data from source if updated will be reflected to the Model Entity

 

 

 

 

 

 

SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

--Insert one liner of record
INSERT INTO "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" VALUES ('C007_DEMO', 'P007_DEMO', 100, 100);
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

--As the jobscheduler is running almost real-time, it reflects right away in showscreen

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

--from the added record, update a line to ensure that UPDATE is working properly. This feature can only work if you have a primary key, in this case customer
UPDATE "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" SET AMOUNT = 999 WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C007_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

--showing that deletion command from source, will not trigger deletion from repliciation table
DELETE FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C007_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Note: The setup we performed is not meant to delete information from the Model Entity.
As being imagined that this will be used as financial application or table, deletion of data record is not advisable.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

--optionally, redo for another set of records, to ensure stability

SELECT * FROM "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;
INSERT INTO "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" VALUES ('C008_DEMO', 'P008_DEMO', 100, 100);
SELECT * FROM "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

UPDATE "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" SET AMOUNT = 999 WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C008_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

DELETE FROM "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C008_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

C2)      OP Replicating to BYOD UM

Now that we are done with replication to SAP PAPM Cloud, Default. Let us setup the replication in SAP PAPM Cloud, BYOD.

In the next steps, the assumption is that the database of SAP PAPM Cloud UM is the same as with the BAS linked Database which will give you less steps to perform and avoid remote sourcing to target table.

 

C2.1)  In the target (BYOD db) perform below statements

In the BAS linked database, login as the HANA DB Service User and perform below HANA SQL commands to ensure that your ServiceInstance user has enough privilege to maintain SAP PAPM UM Model Entity. Shall you encounter any privilege issue, revisit Chapter B.2 ServiceInstance user to have rights to adjust SAP_PAPM_UM.Z table

 

 

 

 

 

 

--insert a dummy data to ensure that the InstanceService user has enough rights to insert records
INSERT INTO "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"." Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" (CUSTOMER, PRODUCT, QUANTITY, AMOUNT) VALUES ('TEST', 'TEST', 0, 0);

--check result
SELECT * FROM "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME";

--delete record inserted to clear up the table
DELETE FROM "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'TEST'; 

--check result
SELECT * FROM "<SAP_PAPM_UM>"."Z_FLOWGRAPH_FG_ME";

 

 

 

 

 

 


C2.2)  From BAS, create Synonym for the ME Table

Since the target table is outside the HDI container, it is important to create in BAS a synonym for this Model Entity table.

Take note though that an issue could occur like below if you skipped performing the steps under B4) HDI Container user to have access to SAP PAPM UM Model Entity

C2.3)  From BAS, create Flowgraph FG_BYOD01

Same steps as with the flowgraph creation in C1.5) From BAS, create flowgraph FG_RT01. The only difference is the target table where we are pointing to replicate the source information. In BYOD, the Model Entity table is not a virtual table unlike in the runtime database approach, since the BAS linked database is the same as with SAP PAPM UM's database

C2.4)  From BAS, Build a JobScheduler

Same as with the job scheduler creation in C1.7) From BAS, Build a JobScheduler. Just this time I used 5 seconds to test a different frequency. As a friendly reminder, same note still applies

Note: The scheduling of cron should be evaluated by your database administrator. As this is just a demo, I used 5seconds. In reality, you would want to schedule this every 15minutes or every hour depending on how frequent your source table is being updated, and how much datarecords the table has.

 


C2.5)  Perform some live checks

Same as with the flowgraph creation in C1.8 Perform some live checks. I will always advise you to check with small amout of data if you can insert, update, and if deletion is not happening.

 

 

 

 

 

 

SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;
INSERT INTO "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" VALUES ('C007_DEMO', 'P007_DEMO', 100, 100);
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

UPDATE "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" SET AMOUNT = 999 WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C007_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

DELETE FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C007_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

INSERT INTO "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" VALUES ('C008_DEMO', 'P008_DEMO', 100, 100);
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

UPDATE "C5144919"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" SET AMOUNT = 999 WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C008_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

DELETE FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" WHERE CUSTOMER = 'C008_DEMO';
SELECT * FROM "SOURCESCHEMA"."O_PECINPUT_WKEY" ORDER BY CUSTOMER ASC;

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

That's all it. Mission completed! (^^,)

I hope this blogpost helped you get new technical knowledge related to Business Application Studio, Flowgraph, and ofcourse Data Replication straight to your SAP PAPM UM's Model Entity!

Until we meet again for another technical blogpost!

 

Have a wonderful data replication moment and modeling time in the cloud!

As a young woman and a first-generation college student, I am on a journey to break into the tech industry. entry.title: Unable to connect to SAP GUI running on abap cloud developer trial docker
entry.content:
Disclaimer: I am new to SAP. I am an experienced Linux developer trying to create a connector with SAP. 

I found the guide https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial showing how to run ABAPTRIAL_2022 in my local machine using docker. I was able to follow the guide and my container is running locally, with the right ports open, with updated license, but I can't seem to connect to any of the ports - I keep getting `connection cancelled` or `connection refused` from my browser. 

Here are the logs of my container start up:

```

3.4s

```

In my `/etc/hosts` file in the host computer, as oriented, I have added an entry to `vhcala4hci` pointing to localhost:

```

ping vhcala4hci
PING vhcala4hci (127.0.0.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from localhost (127.0.0.1): icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.021 ms

```

However, if I try to connect using CURL or the browser to SAP GUI port, I just get an empty response from the server:

 

```

curl -X GET http://vhcala4hci:3200/
curl: (52) Empty reply from server

```

If I try to connect from the browser to `http://vhcala4hci:50000/`, I get an HTML showing 404 error. 

I found some logs inside the container in the folder `/usr/sap/A4H/D00/log/`, but I am not sure what I am looking for in there. I am not sure whether I should be running SAP GUI in a different way or what I could be doing wrong. 

 

Could anyone please assist?

entry.title: Re: How to append an existing custom structure to a stand...

entry.title: HCM Brasil - eSocial S-1.3 - Entrega do evento S-1210 - Pagamentos de Rendimentos do Trabalho
entry.content:
Olá pessoal, 

Na última sexta-feira, 04 de outubro, concluímos a segunda etapa das nossas entregas relacionadas ao eSocial S-1.3. 

Nesta fase, nos dedicamos em realizar todas as modificações relacionadas ao evento S-1210 - Pagamentos de Rendimentos do Trabalho, proporcionando aos nossos clientes e parceiros um período maior para testes e adaptação à nova versão do evento. 

Esta mudança permite a retificação das informações do grupo {infoIRComplem} enviadas no ano anterior, em janeiro do próximo ano, sem a obrigatoriedade de reabrir os períodos anteriores (evento S-1298). Este processo é considerado pelo governo como uma retificação da DIRF.  

A recomendação do governo é que esta funcionalidade seja utilizada de forma extraordinária, somente quando as mudanças da informação do grupo {infoIRComplem} não impactar os valores transitados na folha de pagamento. O governo irá considerar como retificação da DIRF o uso do grupo {infoIRComplem/perAnt}.

Na imagem acima, os blocos em azul representam os eventos S-1210 referentes ao ano de 2025 e o bloco laranja representa o evento S-1210 de janeiro de 2026 

Nesta entrega, as seguintes SAP Notes foram liberadas: 

  • 3511711 - eSocial - New subtype in 0035 infotype to indicate rectification periods from S-1210 event.  
  • 3509400 - Prerequisite transformation object for SAP Note 3509401.  
  • 3509401 - eSocial S-1.3 - S-1210 - Conversion class. 
  • 3515907 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3515949.  
  • 3515949 - eSocial S-1.3 - S-1210 - New feature and table view. 
  • 3521569 - eSocial S-1.3 NT 01/2024 - S-1210 - Update Transformation. 
  • 3514497 - eSocial S-1.3 - S-1210 Labor income payments. 

IMPORTANTE: 
É de extrema importância que, no período de apuração janeiro de 2025, impreterivelmente, o envio do evento S-1210 seja realizado na versão S-1.3 do eSocial. Isso garantirá que todos os eventos de 2025 estejam de acordo com versão do eSocial S-1.3 ou superior, possibilitando o uso do evento S-1210 como substituto da DIRF em 2026. 

Atualização do planejamento 

Conforme divulgado neste blogpost, a SAP planeja entregar essa alteração legal em 3 etapas: 

  1. Etapa 1 em 30 de agosto de 2024: Eventos com escopo limitado (são aqueles que dependem exclusivamente de implementação de clientes e parceiros). 
  2. Etapa 2 em 04 de outubro de 2024: Alterações relacionadas ao evento S-1210 - Pagamentos de Rendimentos do Trabalho. 
  3. Etapa 3 em 01 de novembro de 2024: Alterações dos demais eventos cobertos pela solução standard.

Mais informações 

Você encontra maiores informações sobre esse planejamento acessando o Regulatory Change Manager. 

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3418378 - eSocial - Overview - 2024. 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. 

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço, 

Ramiro Manoel

entry.title: Re: GR not getting posted through BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE-code, but successfully posted by MIGO transac
entry.content: You probably missed one, so what did you check exactly?

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content: I just tried the link given by @S_Sriram on my ABAP/Sybase system, it works well. What is your issue? Also the same answer here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-unlock-users-using-sql-console-on-sap-netweaver-as-abap-7-5x/ba-p/13463351.

entry.title: How to programmatically switch detail tabs in MIGO
entry.content:
I implemented BADI MB_MIGO_BADI and added a new sub-screen tab called Lot Detail.

The business is requesting that after the PO is entered for a goods receipt that the "Quantity" tab be displayed first versus the tab "Lot Detail".  Can someone point me in the right direction?
